name: Timkat
traditions: Processions carrying replica Arks of the Covenant (tabots); blessing of water; mass baptism; white clothing; three-day celebration with feasting and music.
historicalBackground: Timkat celebrates the Epiphany — the baptism of Jesus in the Jordan River. Ethiopia's unique Orthodox tradition dates to the 4th century Aksumite Kingdom.
description: Ethiopian Orthodox Epiphany celebration with dramatic processions, water blessings, and one of Africa's most colorful religious festivals.
